title: Nullable type comparison should not be redundant
description: Nullable type comparison should not be redundantAnalyze your codeintentionality-logicalreliabilityBugredundantWhy is this an issue?How can I fix it?More InfoCallingGetType()on anullable value typeobject returns the underlying value type. Therefore, comparing the returnedTypeobject totypeof(Nullable<SomeType>)will either throw anNullReferenceExceptionor the result will always betrueorfalseand can be known at compile time.Available In:Catch issues on the fly,in your IDEDetect issues in your GitHub, Azure DevOps Services, Bitbucket Cloud, GitLab repositoriesAnalyze code in youron-premise CI©2025SonarSource Sàrl. recommendation: Review and fix according to the rule guidelines.

logic.triggering_conditions: MethodDeclaration nodes that contain GetType() comparisons to nullable types using typeof(Type?)
logic.reasoning: Detects redundant nullable type comparisons where GetType() is compared to nullable types. GetType() on nullable types returns the underlying value type, so comparing to typeof(nullable) is always false or will throw NullReferenceException. Uses regex to match the specific pattern .GetType() == typeof(Type?) within method bodies while excluding safe contexts.
